other_observers: many others, but I don't recall who; I am trying to track down who found it, but haven't managed it, yet.
description: A Sabine's Gull in typical juvenal plumage, it was a small gull with long wings, the bird's upperparts feathers from crown through lower back were a grayish-brown with contrasting pale fringes and contrasted with white forehead and rump.  The underparts were white, except for grayish-brown sides/flanks.  The eyes, bill, and legs were dark/blackish.  The open upperwing had a distinctive tri-colored pattern of black outer pp and pp covs, white inner pp and pp covs and secondaries and greater secondary covs, and grayish-brown median and lesser secondary covs.   The tail was white and notched, with a black band that was terminal on the outer rectrices and subterminal on the inner rectrices.
